title: Level 599
wikitext:
{{Cosmic Theme}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Mate+SC&family=Single+Day&family=Space+Mono:ital,wght@0,400;0,700;1,400;1,700&display=swap'); html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Space Mono'; } }} {{Background|file=The 7 Trials of Destiny bg.gif}} {{Audio|file=Interstellar Reflection.mp3|volume=1}}
What is a star? Can you touch it? Can you feel it? Can you ''kill'' it? They say stars are fire, but fire runs out of fuel. They say stars are light, but light slowly dims until it fades. They say stars are eternal, but truth be told, they are brittle. Some stars fall, and when they fall, they let out a beckoning call. Some stars flicker, and when they flicker, they carry a whisper. Not words you can understand. But echoes that settle in your chest. You carry them like breadcrumbs. Glowing in the dark. There is a light inside your soul. That light has a name. It is called, '''hope.''' Hope that one day, deep within this wretched place, you will soon find peace in the end. But how truthful do you think you are being to yourself? In these halls, in these walls, peace is but a mirage. A lie told to keep you moving. The halls stretch on like constellations, patterns too vast for you to see. Every turn is another orbit, yet still, you spiral inward. The stars above do not guide you. They do not know your name. They burn, they fade, they vanish— just as you will. And still, you walk. And still, you search. For something bright enough, to prove the dark is deceiving. Do you honestly think it will get you what you want? That the light ahead is different from the one left behind? That they hold anything but more silence? You chase, and you chase. And in the end, the dark smiles back. Do you even know what you are searching for? Is it a shining star to guide you? Is it a looming light to save you? Or is it proof that you aren’t already gone?
{{Collapse|num=1|initial=
'''''Are you a star?'''''
|final=
'''''Are you a star?'''''
|body= {{Blurquote|image=The 7 Trials of Destiny bg.gif|quote-text=
''"When we look out into space, we are looking into our own origins, because we are truly children of the stars."''
|person=Brian Cox}}
» Level 599 «
» INTERSTELLAR ASCENSION «
{{Class Custom Image|class=Undetermined|bg-image=Starre.jpg|safety=Mysterious Properties|security=Unknown Information|entity=Unknown Entities|hexagon=
  ✎ '''Overview'''
'''Level 599''' is a vast network of futuristic walkways suspended above the surface of a colossal star. Each path is constructed of smooth, metallic panels lined with railings that hum with a soft electric tone. These walkways intersect and diverge with each other, forming a fragmented lattice-web structure with no visible supports, appearing to float on its own. Below the walkways lies the star known as '''Hymorphia''', a bluish-purple supergiant whose light floods the entire expanse with an ambient, pulsating glow. The atmosphere of the level is silent, except for a constant-low resonance believed to be emanating from the star, and produced by the level itself. '''Hymorphia’s''' surface swirls and bubbles like slow-moving liquid fire, yet the star remains eerily calm. It poses no threat to wanderers. That is… unless '''''it''''' happens. Despite the pathways overwhelmingly close proximity to the star, wanderers claim to experience no extreme heat, no radiation burns, and no signs of increased gravity. The reason for this seemingly impossible occurrence remains unknown. It is believed that the paths and surrounding space exist within a contained ''anomalous bubble'', a sealed distortion separating them from '''Hymorphia’s''' true heat and gravitational pull. However, should a wanderer fall from the path, this protection vanishes. For a brief moment, they may glimpse the star’s surface from within the distortion. But once outside its influence, their body succumbs instantly to '''Hymorphia’s''' heat, gravity, and collapse. Still, the bubble holds strong. And everything remains safe... except when '''''it''''' happens. Though '''Level 599''' appears to be exposed to the vacuum of space, breathable '''oxygen''' is inexplicably present throughout the walkways, and within all interior areas. There is no indication of an artificial atmosphere being produced in the level. Hence, this presence of oxygen is believed to be connected to the same anomalous bubble that protects wanderers from '''Hymorphia’s''' extreme heat and gravity. A handful of collected samples predicts an air composition that somewhat matches Earth, is slightly metallic, and carries a faint scent of ozone.
  ✎ '''Station Interiors'''
Scattered through the walkways of '''Level 599''' are structures resembling a futuristic space station, most often where multiple walkways converge into nodes. They appear to resemble observatories or research labs, though most have long-since lost functionality. The structures are either cylindrical or hexagonal, and connect to the main paths by futuristic airlock style doors that no longer seal properly. Subsections of these structures are as followed: ◇ '''Observation Corridors''': Long glass walled hallways peering onto '''Hymorphia’s''' surface, tinted bluish-gold. The glass panels are reinforced with metallic lattice patterns. ◇ '''Control Rooms''': Circular chambers that contains clusters of inactive machinery, terminals, and display modules. Despite their futuristic appearance, none appear to respond to manual interaction. Occasionally, a display module may flicker on for a brief moment, showing unreadable symbols or magnified views of '''Hymorphia’s''' surface. ◇ '''Dormitory Units''': Small rooms containing compact bunk-beds and storage compartments. All beds remain empty, though impressions remain in the bedding. Some drawers contain occasional metallic ID tags, though most are engraved with random strings of letters and numbers, such as "131-AEF". The storage compartments, though often empty, may contain trace objects such as [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]]. ◇ '''Viewing Decks''': Open circular platforms accessed by a stairwell, and surrounded by transparent barriers and neon-purple railings. They provide clear views of '''Hymorphia’s''' surface, and the walkways above it. Wanderers describe feeling a deep, unexplained emotional response, a mix of overwhelming awe and existential dread. However, clinging onto the railings may become dangerous when '''''it''''' happens...
  ✎ '''The Starborn'''
[[File:Starnorm.png|center|400px]] Despite there being a lack of entity sitings, one species has been observed to be native to this level, '''The Starborn'''. '''The Starborn''' appear as tall, radiant humanoids with translucent skin glowing violet from deep within. Their eyes emit a faint ray of light, and their veins shimmer like constellations in the cosmos. Despite their ethereal beauty, their bodies regenerate slowly upon being damaged. '''The Starborn''' are normally calm and silent, often wandering around, or simply gazing at '''Hymorphia''' and the ceiling of the station nodes. They rarely attack unless provoked, and their presence distorts nearby light sources and induces a state of tranquility among wanderers.
  ✎ '''Entrances and Exits'''
Entrances Entering a flickering airlock door in certain levels while the ambient lights dim to a vibrant violet hue may transport one here. Also, falling through the floor in certain metallic hallways may cause the wanderer to black out and appear on one of the walkways. Exits The most well known exit is via an area dubbed the '''Astra-Probe Node''', a rarely appearing, isolated structure. It resembles an ancient orbital space probe, its hull covered in neon purple runes. If a wanderer manages to enter, the system will power on, an interface will display "Transport Sequenced: Initiated". To which, the probe will soon take off, and after a few seconds, the interior fills with white light as the wanderer is transported to '''[[Level 700]]''' near a destroyed copy of the probe. [[File:EVEN BIGGER AND EVEN BLEUER STAR.gif|center|400px]] {{Blurquote|image=The 7 Trials of Destiny bg.gif|quote-text=
''"It really is beautiful to look into the horizon and watch the mesmerizing glow of the star below your feet. But, when it gets to be unstable, staring might be the last thing you want to do."''
|person=Anonymous}} {{Collapse|num=2|initial=[[File:Starnorm.png|center|400px]]|final=[[File:Starbad.png|thumb|center|400px]]|body=
... "When the light trembles, the star sings in agony." They say it split open on that day. Ribbons of violet fire cutting through the vacuum. Entire nodes fell silent, their cries drowned by the waves of blinding light. Those who witnessed it claim it to have been the worst out of all the others. And then when it faded, all that was left.. was silence, and the ghosts of what once was.
Phenomenon 228:
'''Hymorphian Quakes'''
At unpredictable intervals, '''Level 599''' may experience a rare but devastating occurrence known as a '''Hymorphian Quake'''. This is an event in which the surface of '''Hymorphia''' violently fractures and releases intense flares of radiation. In the minutes following an impending quake, the atmosphere of '''Level 599''' may undergo subtle, but alarming changes. For example, the normally soft resonating hum distorts resembles a metallic ringing in the ears. The color of the star will also deepen from its normal blueish-purple to a vibrant violet. Most importantly, faint small cracks of light begin to appear on the surface of '''Hymorphia''', fissures of pulsating radiant energy. For the best chance at surviving these events, it is recommended for one to locate the nearest station-node structure as soon as noticing the early signs of an impending quake. When the event actually begins, the cracks burst open, releasing plumes of plasma and gas that surge upward. The walkways would shake violently, and temperature and gravity spikes, enough to make movement almost impossible without effort. Once the quake subsides, the cracks seal themselves, and the level returns to its previous state as if nothing occurred. They typically only last for several minutes to even an hour. In the silence after the event, faint burn marks lie on the underside of the walkways, disappearing after a few minutes.
  ✎ '''The Great Quake'''
In a time centuries before '''The Starborn''' was discovered, '''Level 599''' was once inhabited by a theoretical species known as '''Hymorphians''', named after the titular star in this level. Evidence of their existence is found through alien nodes and translated log fragments that have been scavenged. The '''Hymorphians''' are thought to have constructed the network of observation structures from the walkways. This ancient civilization persisted for centuries. That is, until the occurrence of what ancient records describe as '''The Great Quake'''. Unlike typical events, this one was catastrophic, far stronger than any other recorded. Massive bursts of energy tore through the protective distortion, engulfing the structures and instantly vaporizing most of the '''Hymorphians''' in mere seconds. Only a handful survived, protected by the deeper nodes in the station. Over time, these survivors adapted to the changed environment, and their biology began to shift amidst the newly re-forming distortion field. Centuries later, these altered descendents inhabit the level, but are no longer entirely recognizable to their ancestors. Now, they appear as luminous humanoid silhouettes, their behavior largely non aggressive. They are collectively known as '''The Starborn'''.
Oh gentle star... the one who brings light to our lives, the one who guides us... Why.. why the sudden moment of agony? '''Why must you march us blindly to an end..?''' {{Collapse|num=3|initial='''''> ... <'''''|final='''''> ... <'''''|body= But why must ''you'' pretend to show concern? {{USERNAME}}, you know not how it feels. [[Level 599/What Remains of a Star|You know not what it's like when hundreds of lights go dark...]] =='''''
